behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection + CLI execution. Behavior phrase requiring escalation: "Renamed files" in a real "git repository" can depend on actual git status/diff output and rename heuristics; mocking parser inputs can hide misclassification from real command output format. REAL-INFRA is yes (real repo filesystem + CLI/subprocess). Rejected higher levels: no behavior text mentions AI/LLM decisions or multi-agent sequencing, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit baseline, perform a rename (git mv), run the CLI as a subprocess, parse stdout report, and assert renamed count increments while added/deleted counts do not include that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior phrase requiring escalation: "Diffs that include binary files" and "do not cause the tool to crash" implies real binary content handling across git + CLI I/O. Mock text fixtures can miss real byte/encoding/subprocess edge cases. REAL-INFRA is yes (filesystem binary blobs, real git diff behavior, CLI process). Rejected higher levels: no AI/LLM output dependency and no multi-step agent pipeline in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real repo, commit baseline, add/modify a true binary file (e.g., random bytes), run CLI subprocess, assert exit code is 0 and report includes binary file in appropriate category/count.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Behavior phrase requiring escalation: "repository that has no commits yet" often triggers real git HEAD/revision errors that only appear with actual repo state and command execution. Mocking git responses may hide true startup failure modes. REAL-INFRA is yes (real repo state + CLI/subprocess). Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely git/CLI, with no AI/LLM or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Create a fresh git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess against it, assert successful exit and structured empty report on stdout (not traceback/error).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Behavior phrase requiring escalation: "match the actual change history of the repository" is end-to-end correctness against real git history semantics. In-process fakes can diverge from actual git behavior and hide failures in history traversal, diff range selection, or category mapping. REAL-INFRA is yes (real repo/history + CLI execution). Rejected higher levels: no AI/LLM content judgment or chained agentic decisions in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across commits, run CLI subprocess for the target window, and assert reported counts exactly equal expected counts derived from the scripted history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Behavior phrase requiring escalation: "identical whether ... staged or unstaged" depends on real git index vs working tree semantics, which are OS/repo-state behaviors at CLI/runtime boundaries. Mocks can hide discrepancies in how staged/unstaged diffs are queried. REAL-INFRA is yes (real git index/worktree + subprocess CLI). Rejected higher levels: no AI/LLM dependency and no multi-agent workflow in the behavior.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent change sets once staged and once unstaged, run the CLI subprocess in both states, normalize deterministic fields, and assert stdout reports are identical.
